from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Generate a natural‑language description from a list of triples.

    The function builds a readable sentence using simple templates for
    common predicates.  When possible it creates a fluent phrase such as
    “X was built by Y” instead of a raw “X builder Y”.  All phrases are
    combined into one sentence with proper commas and a final period.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple templates for a more natural phrasing
    templates = {
        "location": "{subj} is located in {obj}",
        "owner": "{subj} is owned by {obj}",
        "builder": "{subj} was built by {obj}",
        "completionDate": "{subj} was completed on {obj}",
        "launchSite": "{subj} was launched from {obj}",
        "maidenFlight": "{subj} had its maiden flight on {obj}",
        "finalFlight": "{subj} had its final flight on {obj}",
        "status": "{subj} is {obj}",
        "shipPower": "{subj} has power {obj}",
        "topSpeed": "{subj} can reach a top speed of {obj}",
        "operator": "{subj} is operated by {obj}",
        "manufacturer": "{subj} was manufactured by {obj}",
        "engine": "{subj} is equipped with {obj}",
        "transmission": "{subj} uses a {obj} transmission",
        "wheelbase": "{subj} has a wheelbase of {obj}",
        "length": "{subj} is {obj} long",
        "shipBeam": "{subj} has a beam of {obj}",
        "shipDraft": "{subj} has a draft of {obj}",
        "shipDisplacement": "{subj} displaces {obj}",
        "powerType": "{subj} uses {obj} as its power type",
        "shipClass": "{subj} belongs to the {obj} class",
        "alternativeName": "{subj} is also known as {obj}",
        "relatedMeanOfTransportation": "{subj} is related to {obj}",
        "fate": "{subj} became {obj}",
        "successor": "{subj} was succeeded by {obj}",
        "parentCompany": "{subj} is a subsidiary of {obj}",
        "founder": "{subj} was founded by {obj}",
        "foundationPlace": "{subj} was founded in {obj}",
        "capital": "{subj} has its capital at {obj}",
        "leader": "{subj} is led by {obj}",
    }

    phrases = []
    for t in triples:
        # Clean object value (remove surrounding quotes if present)
        obj = t.object.strip()
        if (obj.startswith('"') and obj.endswith('"')) or (obj.startswith("'") and obj.endswith("'")):
            obj = obj[1:-1]

        tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            phrase = tmpl.format(subj=t.subject, obj=obj)
        else:
            # Default fallback: make predicate readable
            pred = t.predicate.replace("_", " ")
            phrase = f"{t.subject} {pred} {obj}"
        phrases.append(phrase)

    # Combine phrases into a single fluent sentence
    if len(phrases) == 1:
        sentence = phrases[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1] + "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

Change 1: Replace:
  def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
      """Generate a natural‑language description from a list of triples.
  
      The function creates a clause for each triple in the form
      \"<subject> <predicate> <object>\" and joins them into a single
      sentence.  Predicates are human‑readable (underscores are replaced
      with spaces).  If only one triple is supplied, a simple sentence is
      returned; otherwise the clauses are separated by semicolons and the
      whole text ends with a period.
      """
      if not triples:
          return ""
  
      clauses = []
      for t in triples:
          # Make predicate more readable
          pred = t.predicate.replace("_", " ")
          clauses.append(f"{t.subject} {pred} {t.object}")
  
      if len(clauses) == 1:
          return f"{clauses[0]}."
      else:
          return "; ".join(clauses) + "."
